reasoning: There are multiple reports from early Q1 2025 (e.g., KED Global, Army Recognition, Janes, and Overt Defense) stating that Vietnam and South Korea's Hanwha Aerospace are finalizing or have already signed a $276M-$300M contract for 20-30 K9 Thunder self-propelled howitzers. An Army Recognition report from March 2025 even states the contract was 'signed' and 'unveiled in January 2025'. Because the negotiations have clearly progressed to the very final stages or have actually completed, the likelihood of a formal government announcement prior to the July 1, 2025 deadline is very high. The only remaining uncertainty is whether official government channels (e.g., DAPA or Vietnam's Ministry of National Defense) will formally announce the agreement by the stipulated date, or if it will remain technically unannounced/undisclosed due to geopolitical sensitivities or scheduling delays. Given the high degree of public reporting and progress, a resolution to YES is highly probable.
